→ plot:

Andrzej had started getting into metal as a teenager and young adult, growing up neglected and outcasted by the people around him— where he only found solace through music. Especially the dark, edgy themes of DSBM (depressive suicidal black metal) through Bethlehem’s 1996 album, “Dictius Te Necare”, that he found through a small records shops. Where later on, he met Sixten, a likeminded guy with a new interest in the genre, but unsure of where to start.

He started a band with Sixten, “Mors Certa, Hora Incerta”, where he’d do all sorts of gigs in underground venues or shitty basements. Where then, he met you, his muse. Andrzej finds a purpose in living— with you around. But like every normal couple (or at least what he thinks is normal) arguments happen, and it’s never anything bad— except this time, you slapped him. Yet you still let him fuck you after.
